stories.title: Cristopher Sánchez's Historic Scoreless Streak Ends at 50 2/3 — Fifth-Longest in MLB History
stories.angle: The streak is over — and the legacy is secured. 50 2/3 innings without allowing a run, fifth-longest in baseball history, longest ever by a left-hander. The fans who were there Wednesday night knew exactly what they were watching.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Cristopher Sánchez: 50⅔ scoreless innings. Ended Wednesday.

All-time (since 1893):
1. Hershiser — 59 IP
2. Drysdale — 58 IP
3. W. Johnson — 55⅔ IP
4. Coombs — 53 IP
5. Sánchez — 50⅔ IP

Longest ever by a LHP. #Phillies

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Cristopher Sánchez's streak ended at 50 2/3 innings. The fifth-longest in baseball history.

But the Phillies still won 3-2. And CBP gave him a standing ovation that lasted over a minute.

The streak is over. The legacy isn't. #Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Cristopher Sánchez's 50 2/3-Inning Scoreless Streak Is the Fifth-Longest in Baseball History

research.facts:
Cristopher Sánchez Scoreless Streak Ends
**Confirmed facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Streak ended Wednesday night vs San Diego Padres at Citizens Bank Park
- Jackson Merrill hit RBI single in 7th inning to plate Ty France
- Streak ended at 50 2/3 consecutive scoreless innings
- 5th-longest streak in MLB history since 1893 (mound at current distance)
- All-time order: Hershiser 59 IP (1988), Drysdale 58 IP (1968), Walter Johnson 55 2/3 IP (1913), Jack Coombs 53 IP (1910), Sánchez 50 2/3 (2026)
- Longest consecutive scoreless innings streak by any left-handed pitcher in MLB history
- Phillies franchise record (broke Grover Cleveland Alexander's 1911 mark at 44 2/3)
- Phillies won 3-2 despite streak ending
- Sánchez finished 7 innings, 1 run, 8 strikeouts
- ERA lowered to 1.46 after the game
- Citizens Bank Park gave standing ovation lasting more than a minute
research.sources:
- Sources: mlb.com, yahoo sports, nbcsportsphiladelphia, cbsnews philadelphia, espn
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Season record approximately 11-4 (updated from 5-2 in early season)
- Season K total approximately 144 (from mid-July search; 3rd in MLB)
- WHIP approximately 1.19
**LOW — do not use:**
- Any specific Padres player stats beyond Merrill RBI single

stories.title: Brad Keller's UCL Tear Turns Phillies' Bullpen Need Into a Deadline Emergency
stories.angle: A team that was already shopping for bullpen help just lost its setup man to Tommy John surgery. The Phillies' trade deadline just became a bullpen-first emergency with 17 days to act.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Brad Keller is out for the season.

Torn UCL. Likely Tommy John surgery. 12-14 months of recovery.

The Phillies were already in the market for bullpen help. Now it's urgent.

August 3 deadline: 17 days. #Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Brad Keller's UCL Tear Turned the Phillies' Trade Deadline Into a Bullpen Emergency

research.facts:
Brad Keller UCL Tear
**Confirmed facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Brad Keller placed on 15-day IL with torn UCL (right elbow)
- Retroactive to July 13
- Season-ending injury; will likely undergo Tommy John surgery
- Recovery timeline: 12-14 months for Tommy John
- Keller age: 30
- Second opinion expected before surgery
- Seth Johnson recalled from minors to take roster spot
- Injury announced July 16, 2026
- Phillies were already in market for bullpen help before deadline
- Keller's absence moves leverage reliever ahead of RH bat and back-end starter on Phillies' priority list
- August 3 trade deadline = 17 days from July 17
research.sources: - Sources: cbssports, mlb.com phillies, inquirer, mlbtraderumors, lastwordonsports, yardbarker, phillyvoice

stories.title: Tarik Skubal Gives Tigers an Ultimatum: "Play Better Baseball or Else"
stories.angle: Skubal is giving the Tigers a window — a real one, with a closing date. "Play better baseball or else" is not a threat. It's a deadline within a deadline. The Tigers have 17 days.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal on the Detroit Tigers heading into the August 3 deadline:

"We need to play better baseball or else."

He doesn't want to be traded. But "or else" is doing a lot of work in that sentence.

17 days left. #Tigers #MLB

article.headline: Tarik Skubal Just Gave the Detroit Tigers a Deadline Within a Deadline

research.facts:
Tarik Skubal Trade Situation
**Confirmed facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Two-time AL Cy Young Award winner (2024 unanimous all 30 votes, 2025)
- Contract year; $32M salary (arbitration 2026)
- Extension talks described as "nil"
- Does NOT want to be traded; told others he wants Tigers to be buyers
- Quote: "We need to play better baseball or else" (per ESPN, Yahoo Sports)
- Returned from bone chip surgery; fastball at 99.9 mph (surgery concerns answered)
- 85% estimated trade probability
- Best fits: Dodgers, Yankees, Braves, Brewers, Blue Jays, Rays
- August 3 trade deadline
- Expected FA ask: $400M+
research.sources:
- Sources: si.com, yahoo sports, espn, si.com tigers, sportsnaut, bleacherreport
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Yankees and Phillies previously identified as frontrunners (may have changed)
- Tigers 19-12 since June 1 (referenced in July 14 content)

stories.title: The 2026 MLB Trade Deadline Is August 3 — Here's What the Market Looks Like
stories.angle: The second half is here and the deadline is 17 days away. Every team in baseball is about to have their direction defined by what they do or don't do before August 3.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
MLB trade deadline: August 3. 17 days.

Top names:
• Skubal — 85% chance of being traded
• Arraez — 90%
• Chapman (BOS) — ~90%

Buyers: Phillies, Brewers, Cubs, Rays.
Sellers: Mets, Padres, A's.

#MLB #TradeDeadline

article.headline: The 2026 MLB Trade Deadline Is 17 Days Away — Here's the Full Market Picture

research.facts:
MLB Trade Deadline Overview
**Confirmed facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- August 3, 2026 at 6 PM ET = MLB trade deadline
- Top names: Skubal (85%), Luis Arraez (90%), Aroldis Chapman (Boston, ~90%)
- Also available: Sandy Alcantara, Reid Detmers, Sonny Gray, Kevin Gausman, Joe Ryan, Michael Wacha, Wandy Peralta
- Buyers: Dodgers, Brewers, Cubs, Phillies (urgent after Keller), Rays, Red Sox
- Sellers: Mets (fired Mendoza, confirmed sellers), Padres (Mason Miller available), Athletics, Royals, Angels, Reds, Giants
- NL Central surprise buyers potentially: Marlins, Cardinals, Nationals, Pirates
- Nationals traded Robert Hassell III to Pirates for cash (early seller signal)
research.sources: - Sources: cbssports, bleacherreport, yahoo sports, mlb.com, foxsports

stories.title: MLB Second Half, Day 2 — Standings, Races, and What's Changed
stories.angle: Day 2 of the second half. Standings are set. Every team knows where they are. The only question now is what they're going to do about it before August 3.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
MLB second half standings, Day 2:

NL: Dodgers 61-36 | Brewers 59-37
AL: Rays 56-38 | White Sox 50-45 | Rangers 49-47

The White Sox went 41-121 in 2024. They're leading the AL Central.

17 days to the deadline. #MLB
